seoMetaTitle: Bronze Tech Products | Board-to-Board, Wire-to-Board, Circular Connectors | LiTong
seoMetaDescription: Browse Bronze Tech product portfolio including board-to-board connectors, wire-to-board connectors, circular connectors, and custom interconnect solutions. Technical specifications available.

faqs.question: What product categories does Bronze Tech offer?
faqs.answer: Bronze Tech offers four main product categories: 1) Board-to-Board Connectors - High-density connectors for compact PCB designs with pitches ranging from 0.8mm to 5.08mm, supporting signal and power applications. 2) Wire-to-Board Connectors - Reliable connections for wire harnesses with crimp, IDC, and screw terminal options for various wire gauges. 3) Circular Connectors - Rugged industrial-grade connectors with IP67/IP68 protection, available in M8, M12, M16, and M23 sizes. 4) Custom Interconnect Solutions - Tailored connector designs for specific application requirements including special materials, plating, and form factors. All products meet international standards and are RoHS compliant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore each product category to find detailed specifications, 3D models, and application notes for your specific interconnect needs.

faqs.question: How do I choose between different Bronze Tech connector series?
faqs.answer: Choosing the right Bronze Tech connector series depends on your application requirements: 1) BTB Series - High-density board-to-board with 1.27mm pitch for compact designs. 2) WTB Series - Wire-to-board with various termination options for flexible integration. 3) CIR Series - Circular connectors with IP67 protection for harsh environments. 4) HD Series - High-current connectors for power applications up to 50A. Consider factors like current rating, voltage, environmental conditions, mating cycles, and space constraints when selecting. Our FAE team can provide selection guidance based on your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selector tool or contact our FAE team for personalized recommendations based on your application specifications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between board-to-board and wire-to-board connectors?
faqs.answer: Board-to-board (BTB) and wire-to-board (WTB) connectors serve different purposes: BTB connectors directly connect two PCBs together, providing the shortest signal path and highest density. They are ideal for stacking boards or connecting parallel boards in compact designs. WTB connectors connect wires or cables to a PCB, providing flexibility for external connections and field installation. They support various wire gauges and termination methods (crimp, IDC, screw). BTB connectors typically have higher pin counts and density, while WTB connectors offer more flexibility for system integration and maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose BTB for compact inter-board connections, WTB for flexible external connections and field serviceability.

categories.name: Board-to-Board Connectors
categories.description: Bronze Tech board-to-board connectors provide high-density interconnections between PCBs with pitches ranging from 0.8mm to 5.08mm. These connectors feature reliable contact designs, high mating cycle durability, and excellent signal integrity for both signal and power applications. Available in vertical and right-angle configurations with various stacking heights.
categories.longDescription: Bronze Tech board-to-board connectors are designed for high-density PCB interconnections in compact electronic devices. The product range includes mezzanine connectors, stacking connectors, and edge card connectors with pitches from 0.8mm to 5.08mm. These connectors feature precision-machined contacts with gold plating for reliable connections, supporting current ratings from 1A to 15A per contact. The series offers various mounting options including SMT, through-hole, and press-fit, with stacking heights from 3mm to 40mm. All connectors are RoHS compliant and meet UL, CSA, and IEC standards. Typical applications include industrial controls, medical devices, telecommunications equipment, and automotive electronics.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Bronze Tech Board-to-Board Connectors
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right board-to-board connector for your PCB design

faqs.question: What pitch options are available for Bronze Tech board-to-board connectors?
faqs.answer: Bronze Tech offers board-to-board connectors in multiple pitch options: 0.8mm for ultra-compact designs, 1.0mm for high-density signal applications, 1.27mm for standard density, 2.0mm for mixed signal/power, 2.54mm for industrial applications, and 5.08mm for high-current power applications. The 0.8mm and 1.0mm pitches are ideal for portable devices and wearables, while 2.54mm and 5.08mm are preferred for industrial and automotive applications requiring higher current capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose smaller pitches (0.8-1.27mm) for compact designs, larger pitches (2.54-5.08mm) for power applications.

faqs.question: How do I determine the stacking height for board-to-board connectors?
faqs.answer: Stacking height selection depends on your PCB spacing requirements: 1) Measure the distance between PCBs including components and clearance. 2) Consider the tallest component on either board. 3) Add safety margin for tolerances (typically 0.5-1mm). 4) Select standard stacking height from 3mm to 40mm. Bronze Tech offers standard heights at 3, 5, 8, 10, 12, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, and 40mm. Custom heights are available for volume orders. Always verify the mating length and wipe distance meet your reliability requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Measure PCB spacing, add component clearance and safety margin, then select nearest standard height.

products.name: BTB-27 Series 10-Pin Vertical
products.description: 1.27mm pitch 10-pin vertical board-to-board connector with 5mm stacking height for compact PCB designs
products.shortDescription: 1.27mm pitch 10-pin vertical connector with 5mm stacking height for signal applications
descriptionParagraphs: The BTB-2710-V-05 is a 1.27mm pitch board-to-board connector designed for high-density PCB interconnections.
This vertical connector features precision-machined phosphor bronze contacts with 0.76μm gold plating for reliable connections and excellent corrosion resistance.
The 5mm stacking height is ideal for compact designs while providing adequate clearance for standard SMT components.
features: Vacuum pick-up cap for automated assembly

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Interconnect Systems
faeReview.content: The BTB-27 series is my go-to recommendation for standard-density board-to-board applications. The 1.27mm pitch offers an excellent balance between density and manufacturability. The gold plating thickness of 0.76μm provides reliable connections even in harsh environments. I particularly like the polarization feature which prevents costly mis-mating errors during assembly. For high-vibration applications, recommend adding a locking clip accessory.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 1.27mm pitch connector with excellent signal integrity

alternativeParts.reason: Higher stacking height for increased component clearance
alternativeParts.useCase: Designs with taller components on PCB

alternativeParts.reason: More positions for higher pin count requirements

companionParts.description: Right-angle version for parallel board mounting

faqs.question: What is the current rating of BTB-2710-V-05?
faqs.answer: The BTB-2710-V-05 is rated for 1A per contact with all contacts powered. This rating is based on a 20°C temperature rise above ambient at sea level. For higher current requirements, consider using multiple contacts in parallel or selecting the BTB-50 series with larger contacts. Always verify the temperature rise under your specific operating conditions and ensure adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1A rating for signal applications. For power, parallel multiple contacts or select higher current series.

faqs.question: How many mating cycles can BTB-2710-V-05 withstand?
faqs.answer: The BTB-2710-V-05 is rated for a minimum of 500 mating cycles while maintaining electrical and mechanical specifications. This rating assumes proper mating alignment and clean operating conditions. For applications requiring higher cycle counts, consider the BTB-27-HD series rated for 1000+ cycles. To maximize connector life, avoid angular mating, ensure proper alignment guides are used, and keep contacts clean from contamination.
faqs.decisionGuide: 500 cycles sufficient for most applications. For frequent mating, consider HD series with 1000+ cycles.

faqs.question: What is the recommended PCB layout for BTB-2710-V-05?
faqs.answer: Recommended PCB layout for BTB-2710-V-05: 1) Pad size: 0.76mm x 1.52mm for 1.27mm pitch. 2) Keep-out area: 2.0mm from connector body for mating clearance. 3) Via placement: Place vias behind pads for signal routing. 4) Copper pour: Avoid copper directly under connector to prevent shorting. 5) Solder mask: 0.05mm clearance from pad edges. 6) Silkscreen: Mark pin 1 location clearly. Refer to the datasheet for detailed mechanical drawings and recommended PCB footprints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet recommendations exactly. Use provided PCB footprint library when available.

faqs.question: Is BTB-2710-V-05 suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: The BTB-2710-V-05 meets basic industrial requirements but is not specifically qualified for automotive applications. For automotive use, consider the BTB-27-A series which is AEC-Q200 qualified and tested to automotive temperature cycles (-40°C to +125°C). Automotive applications also require additional validation for vibration, shock, and temperature cycling. Contact our FAE team for automotive-qualified alternatives and application support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BTB-27-A series for automotive. Standard BTB-27 suitable for industrial and commercial.

faqs.question: What plating options are available for BTB-2710-V-05?
faqs.answer: The BTB-2710-V-05 standard plating is 0.76μm gold over 1.27μm nickel on contacts with matte tin on tails. Optional platings include: 1) 1.27μm gold for harsh environments, 2) 0.38μm gold for cost-sensitive applications, 3) Palladium-nickel for high-wear applications. Tin plating is standard on tails, with optional gold flash available. Specify plating requirements when ordering. Lead time may vary for non-standard plating options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard 0.76μm gold suitable for most applications. Increase for harsh environments, decrease for cost savings.

products.name: BTB-50 Series 20-Pin Vertical
products.description: 5.08mm pitch 20-pin vertical board-to-board connector with 10mm stacking height for power applications up to 15A
products.shortDescription: 5.08mm pitch 20-pin power connector with 10mm stacking height for high-current applications
descriptionParagraphs: The BTB-5020-V-10 is a high-power board-to-board connector featuring 5.08mm pitch for increased current capacity.
This connector uses high-conductivity copper alloy contacts with 1.27μm gold plating to support up to 15A per contact.
The robust design and larger contacts make it ideal for power distribution and industrial control applications.
features: 15A per contact rating for power applications
Threaded insert option for mechanical stability

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The BTB-50 series is excellent for power distribution applications. The 5.08mm pitch and heavy-duty contacts handle 15A with minimal temperature rise. I recommend using all contact positions for power to maximize current capacity. The threaded insert option is essential for high-vibration applications like EV charging. For thermal management, ensure adequate copper area (minimum 2oz) on PCB. The 1000+ mating cycle rating is impressive for a power connector.
faeReview.highlight: High-current capacity with robust design for industrial power applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower pin count for simpler power designs
alternativeParts.useCase: Simple power distribution with fewer signals

faqs.question: What is the maximum current for BTB-5020-V-10?
faqs.answer: The BTB-5020-V-10 is rated for 15A per contact with all contacts powered simultaneously. This rating assumes 30°C temperature rise above ambient with adequate PCB copper area (2oz minimum recommended). For higher current, parallel multiple contacts - with all 20 contacts paralleled, the connector can handle 100A+ total. Always verify temperature rise under actual operating conditions and ensure proper heat dissipation through PCB design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 15A per contact for individual circuits. Parallel contacts for higher total current.

faqs.question: What wire gauge can be used with BTB-5020-V-10?
faqs.answer: The BTB-5020-V-10 is a board-to-board connector and does not directly accept wire. For wire connections, use the companion WTB series wire-to-board connectors or terminal blocks. If you need to connect wires to this connector, consider using a PCB-mounted terminal block on the mating board. For direct wire-to-wire connections, the CIR series circular connectors are more appropriate.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use WTB series for wire-to-board, CIR series for wire-to-wire connections.

categories.name: Wire-to-Board Connectors
categories.description: Bronze Tech wire-to-board connectors provide reliable connections between wire harnesses and PCBs. Available with crimp, IDC, and screw terminal options supporting wire gauges from AWG 28 to AWG 12. These connectors feature secure locking mechanisms and are ideal for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.
categories.longDescription: Bronze Tech wire-to-board connectors offer flexible interconnection solutions for wire-to-PCB applications. The product line includes crimp-style connectors for high-volume production, IDC (insulation displacement) connectors for quick field installation, and screw terminal connectors for secure, reusable connections. Wire gauges supported range from AWG 28 (signal) to AWG 12 (power). All connectors feature positive locking mechanisms to prevent accidental disconnection and polarized housings to ensure correct mating orientation. The series supports current ratings from 2A to 30A depending on configuration. Applications include industrial control panels, automotive wiring harnesses, appliance controls, and LED lighting systems.

series.name: WTB-PH Series
series.description: 2.0mm pitch crimp connectors for signal applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Bronze Tech Wire-to-Board Connectors
selectionGuide.description: Guide to choosing the right wire-to-board connector based on wire gauge, current, and application requirements

faqs.question: What wire gauges are supported by Bronze Tech wire-to-board connectors?
faqs.answer: Bronze Tech wire-to-board connectors support a wide range of wire gauges: AWG 28-26 for signal applications (2A), AWG 24-22 for general purpose (5A), AWG 20-18 for power applications (10A), and AWG 16-12 for high-current (15-30A). The specific gauge range depends on the connector series - PH series handles AWG 28-22, XH series handles AWG 26-20, VH series handles AWG 22-16, and EH series handles AWG 18-12. Always verify the wire strip length and crimp tool compatibility for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match wire gauge to connector series: PH for signal, XH for general, VH for power, EH for high-current.

faqs.question: What is the difference between crimp and IDC termination?
faqs.answer: Crimp termination requires stripping wire insulation and crimping a metal terminal onto the conductor, then inserting into the housing. It provides the most reliable connection and is best for high-vibration applications. IDC (Insulation Displacement Connection) uses a sharp blade that pierces the insulation to make contact with the conductor when pressed into the connector. IDC is faster to install and requires no stripping, making it ideal for field repairs and low-volume production. However, crimp connections generally have better long-term reliability and lower contact resistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use crimp for high-reliability and vibration. Use IDC for quick installation and field repairs.

products.name: WTB-XH Series 4-Pin Crimp
products.description: 2.5mm pitch 4-pin wire-to-board crimp connector for general purpose applications up to 7A
products.shortDescription: 2.5mm pitch 4-pin crimp connector for general purpose applications
descriptionParagraphs: The WTB-XH-4P-CR is a versatile 2.5mm pitch wire-to-board connector suitable for a wide range of applications.
This crimp-style connector features tin-plated brass contacts and supports wire gauges from AWG 26 to AWG 20.
The positive locking mechanism ensures secure mating and prevents accidental disconnection.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The WTB-XH series is my top recommendation for general-purpose wire-to-board applications. The 2.5mm pitch strikes a good balance between size and current capacity. The positive lock mechanism provides excellent retention - I've seen these connectors pass 50G shock tests in industrial equipment. The crimp contacts are easy to work with using standard tooling. For high-vibration applications, I recommend adding the optional locking clip. The wide wire gauge range (AWG 26-20) provides flexibility for various applications.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile connector with excellent locking mechanism for industrial use

companionParts.description: Additional locking clip for high vibration

faqs.question: What crimp tool is recommended for WTB-XH-4P-CR?
faqs.answer: The recommended crimp tool for WTB-XH-4P-CR is the Bronze Tech CT-XH-01 hand crimp tool for low-volume production and field service. For high-volume manufacturing, use the CT-XH-AUTO pneumatic crimp tool. Both tools provide consistent crimp quality with proper compression force. The die set for AWG 26-20 is included. Always verify crimp quality by measuring pull force (minimum 5kgf for AWG 24) and inspecting the crimp cross-section.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hand tool for prototyping/low volume. Use pneumatic tool for production.

faqs.question: What is the maximum current for WTB-XH-4P-CR?
faqs.answer: The WTB-XH-4P-CR is rated for 7A per contact with all contacts powered. This rating is based on a 30°C temperature rise above ambient using AWG 20 wire. When using smaller wire (AWG 24-26), the current capacity may be limited by the wire rather than the connector. For higher current applications, consider the WTB-VH series rated for 15A. Always verify the temperature rise under your specific operating conditions and ensure adequate wire gauge for the current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 7A rating with AWG 20. Consider VH series for higher current needs.

faqs.question: Is WTB-XH-4P-CR suitable for outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The standard WTB-XH-4P-CR has a nylon housing rated for indoor use. For outdoor applications, use the WTB-XH-4P-CR-WP version with IP65 rating and weather-resistant housing material. The WP version features additional sealing and UV-resistant housing. For harsh outdoor environments with exposure to chemicals or extreme temperatures, consider the WTB-XH-4P-CR-HE high-environment version with enhanced sealing and extended temperature range (-55°C to +125°C).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard for indoor, WP version for outdoor, HE version for harsh environments.

faqs.question: What is the mating cycle rating for WTB-XH-4P-CR?
faqs.answer: The WTB-XH-4P-CR is rated for 50 mating cycles minimum while maintaining electrical and mechanical specifications. This rating assumes proper mating alignment and clean operating conditions. For applications requiring frequent connection/disconnection, consider the WTB-XH-HD series rated for 200+ cycles. To maximize connector life, ensure the locking mechanism is fully engaged and disengaged during operation - never pull on the wires to disconnect.
faqs.decisionGuide: 50 cycles sufficient for fixed installations. Use HD series for frequent mating.

faqs.question: Can WTB-XH-4P-CR be used with ribbon cable?
faqs.answer: The WTB-XH-4P-CR is designed for discrete wire crimping and is not compatible with ribbon cable. For ribbon cable applications, use the WTB-XH-4P-IDC version with insulation displacement contacts designed for flat ribbon cable. The IDC version supports standard 1.27mm pitch ribbon cable and provides quick, tool-free installation. For discrete wire applications, the crimp version provides better reliability and lower contact resistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use crimp version for discrete wires, IDC version for ribbon cable.

products.name: WTB-VH Series 3-Pin Crimp
products.description: 3.96mm pitch 3-pin wire-to-board crimp connector for power applications up to 15A
products.shortDescription: 3.96mm pitch 3-pin power connector supporting up to 15A per contact
descriptionParagraphs: The WTB-VH-3P-CR is a high-current wire-to-board connector designed for power distribution applications.
The 3.96mm pitch and large contacts support up to 15A per contact with minimal temperature rise.
This connector is ideal for power supplies, motor drives, and industrial control applications.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The WTB-VH series is my go-to for power applications up to 15A. The large contacts and generous pitch provide excellent current capacity with low temperature rise. I've used these in power supplies and motor drives with excellent results. The ramp-style lock is easy to operate while providing secure retention. For best results, use AWG 16 wire for maximum current and ensure proper crimp quality. The connector is compatible with standard VH series headers from multiple manufacturers.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable power connector with excellent current capacity

alternativeParts.reason: Fewer positions for simple power connections

companionParts.description: Crimp tool for VH series contacts

faqs.question: What is the maximum current for WTB-VH-3P-CR?
faqs.answer: The WTB-VH-3P-CR is rated for 15A per contact with all contacts powered simultaneously. This rating assumes AWG 16 wire and 30°C temperature rise. With all three contacts used for power (45A total), ensure adequate wire gauge and PCB copper area for heat dissipation. For continuous high-current operation, I recommend monitoring the connector temperature during validation testing. The connector can handle short-term overloads up to 20A per contact.
faqs.decisionGuide: 15A per contact with AWG 16. Use all contacts for power to maximize capacity.

faqs.question: What wire strip length is required for WTB-VH-3P-CR?
faqs.answer: The recommended wire strip length for WTB-VH-3P-CR is 5.0-5.5mm. This length ensures proper contact between the crimp barrel and conductor while preventing exposed wire outside the insulation crimp. Use a quality wire stripper for consistent results. After crimping, verify that the conductor is visible through the inspection hole in the contact and that no strands are extending beyond the contact. The insulation should be captured by the insulation crimp without being crushed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Strip 5.0-5.5mm of insulation. Verify through inspection hole after crimping.

categories.name: Circular Connectors
categories.description: Bronze Tech circular connectors provide rugged, reliable connections for harsh industrial environments. Available in M8, M12, M16, and M23 sizes with IP67/IP68 protection ratings. These connectors feature metal or plastic housings, multiple pin configurations, and shielding options for signal integrity.
categories.longDescription: Bronze Tech circular connectors are designed for demanding industrial applications requiring robust, environmentally sealed connections. The product range includes M8 and M12 connectors for sensor and actuator applications, M16 connectors for industrial Ethernet and power, and M23 connectors for high-density signal and power applications. All connectors feature IP67 or IP68 protection against dust and water ingress. Options include metal housings for EMI shielding, plastic housings for cost-sensitive applications, various mounting styles (panel mount, cable mount, PCB mount), and multiple coding options to prevent mis-mating. These connectors meet industrial standards including IEC 61076-2-101 and are widely used in factory automation, process control, and transportation applications.

series.name: CIR-M8 Series
series.description: M8 size for sensors and small actuators

series.name: CIR-M12 Series
series.description: M12 size - industry standard for industrial Ethernet

series.name: CIR-M16 Series
series.description: M16 size for power and signal applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Bronze Tech Circular Connectors
selectionGuide.description: Guide to selecting the right circular connector size and configuration for industrial applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between M8, M12, M16, and M23 circular connectors?
faqs.answer: M8 connectors are the smallest (8mm thread) with 3-8 pins, ideal for sensors and compact actuators. M12 connectors (12mm thread) are the industrial standard with 2-17 pins, widely used for sensors, actuators, and industrial Ethernet. M16 connectors (16mm thread) offer more power capacity with 2-24 pins, suitable for power distribution and signal applications. M23 connectors (23mm thread) are the largest with up to 19 pins, designed for high-density signal and high-current power applications. Choose based on pin count needs, current requirements, and space constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: M8 for sensors, M12 for general industrial, M16 for power, M23 for high-density.

faqs.question: What does IP67 and IP68 protection mean for circular connectors?
faqs.answer: IP67 means the connector is dust-tight (6) and protected against temporary immersion in water up to 1 meter for 30 minutes (7). IP68 means dust-tight (6) and protected against continuous immersion in water under conditions specified by the manufacturer (8), typically deeper than 1 meter and longer than 30 minutes. Bronze Tech IP68 connectors are tested to 2 meters for 24 hours. For outdoor applications and washdown environments, IP67 is usually sufficient. For submersible applications, choose IP68 with appropriate depth rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: IP67 for most industrial. IP68 for submersible or extreme washdown.

products.name: CIR-M12 Series 8-Pin Straight
products.description: M12 circular connector with 8 pins in straight configuration, IP67 rated for industrial Ethernet and sensor applications
products.shortDescription: M12 8-pin straight connector with IP67 rating for industrial applications
descriptionParagraphs: The CIR-M12-8P-STR is an industry-standard M12 circular connector designed for industrial automation applications.
This 8-pin configuration supports industrial Ethernet (Profinet, EtherCAT) and high-density sensor connections.
The IP67 rating ensures reliable operation in harsh environments with dust and water exposure.

faeReview.author: James Wilson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Networks
faeReview.content: The CIR-M12 series is essential for modern industrial automation. The 8-pin version is perfect for industrial Ethernet applications - I've used these extensively in Profinet and EtherCAT installations. The IP67 rating holds up well in harsh factory environments. The metal housing provides excellent EMI shielding for reliable data transmission. Always verify the coding (A-code for sensors, D-code for Ethernet) to ensure compatibility. For vibration-prone locations, use the version with locking threads.
faeReview.highlight: Industry-standard connector for industrial Ethernet and automation

alternativeParts.useCase: Tight spaces where straight connector won't fit

comparison.compatibility: Same series, fewer pins, higher current

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capacity with fewer pins

faqs.question: What is the difference between A-coded and D-coded M12 connectors?
faqs.answer: A-coded M12 connectors are the standard coding used for sensors, actuators, and DC power. They have the standard pin arrangement and are the most common type. D-coded M12 connectors are specifically designed for industrial Ethernet (100 Mbps) with a different pin arrangement optimized for data transmission. The coding is indicated by the keying position on the connector - A-code has one key, D-code has a different key position. Never force connectors with different codings together as this will damage the contacts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use A-code for sensors/power. Use D-code for industrial Ethernet.

faqs.question: What cable diameter is compatible with CIR-M12-8P-STR?
faqs.answer: The CIR-M12-8P-STR accepts cables with outer diameter of 4-6mm. This range accommodates standard industrial Ethernet and sensor cables. For cables outside this range, use an adapter or select a different cable gland size. The cable gland is made of TPE material and provides strain relief and environmental sealing. When assembling, ensure the cable jacket is clean and undamaged for proper sealing. For armored cables, the maximum diameter may be reduced.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4-6mm OD cables. Verify cable jacket condition before assembly.

faqs.question: Is CIR-M12-8P-STR suitable for Profibus applications?
faqs.answer: The CIR-M12-8P-STR with 8 pins is designed for industrial Ethernet (Profinet, EtherCAT) and is not compatible with Profibus. For Profibus applications, use the CIR-M12-5P-STR (B-coded) which is specifically designed for Profibus PA and DP. The B-code has a different keying to prevent mis-mating with other M12 connectors. Profibus uses a specific pin assignment that differs from standard sensor wiring, so using the correct B-coded connector is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 8-pin A-code for Ethernet. Use 5-pin B-code for Profibus.

faqs.question: What is the torque specification for CIR-M12-8P-STR?
faqs.answer: The recommended mating torque for CIR-M12-8P-STR is 0.4-0.6 Nm (3.5-5.3 in-lbs). Use a torque wrench with appropriate adapter for proper tightening. Over-tightening can damage the threads or compromise the seal. Under-tightening may result in IP rating failure. The connector should be hand-tight plus approximately 1/4 turn with a wrench. For frequent mating applications, apply a light coating of silicone grease to the threads to prevent galling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Tighten to 0.4-0.6 Nm. Hand-tight plus 1/4 turn.

faqs.question: Can CIR-M12-8P-STR be used in food and beverage applications?
faqs.answer: The standard CIR-M12-8P-STR is suitable for general industrial use but is not specifically designed for food and beverage applications. For food and beverage use, select the CIR-M12-8P-STR-FB version with FDA-compliant materials, smooth surfaces for easy cleaning, and enhanced chemical resistance to cleaning agents. The FB version also features blue coloring for easy visual identification in food processing areas. Both versions maintain IP67 protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard for general industrial. Use FB version for food and beverage.

products.name: CIR-M8 Series 4-Pin Straight
products.description: M8 circular connector with 4 pins in straight configuration, IP67 rated for sensors and compact actuators
products.shortDescription: M8 4-pin straight connector with IP67 rating for sensor applications
descriptionParagraphs: The CIR-M8-4P-STR is a compact M8 circular connector ideal for sensor and actuator connections in tight spaces.
The 4-pin configuration supports power and signal for standard industrial sensors.
The small M8 size saves space while maintaining IP67 protection for harsh environments.

faeReview.author: Lisa Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Sensor Applications
faeReview.content: The CIR-M8 series is perfect for sensor applications where space is limited. The 4-pin version handles power and signal for most industrial sensors. The IP67 rating is genuine - I've tested these in washdown environments without issues. The small size is great for mounting on compact sensors. For best results, use the recommended 4mm cable diameter and ensure proper torque when mating. The M8 size is becoming the standard for compact sensors in automation.
faeReview.highlight: Compact connector ideal for sensor applications in tight spaces

alternativeParts.useCase: Basic sensors requiring only power and signal

faqs.question: What sensors are compatible with CIR-M8-4P-STR?
faqs.answer: The CIR-M8-4P-STR is compatible with most industrial sensors using the standard M8 interface. This includes proximity sensors (inductive, capacitive), photoelectric sensors, magnetic sensors, and compact actuators. The 4-pin configuration typically carries: Pin 1 = +V, Pin 2 = Output 1, Pin 3 = 0V/GND, Pin 4 = Output 2 (for sensors with complementary output). Always verify the sensor datasheet for pin assignment compatibility. The A-coding is standard for sensors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compatible with most M8 sensors. Verify pin assignment in sensor datasheet.

faqs.question: What is the difference between M8 and M12 connectors?
faqs.answer: M8 connectors are smaller (8mm thread diameter) compared to M12 (12mm thread). M8 is used for compact sensors and space-constrained applications, typically with 3-4 pins. M12 is the industry standard for general industrial use with 2-17 pins available. M8 is ideal for small sensors where space is critical, while M12 offers more pin options and higher current capacity. Both provide IP67 protection. M8 is becoming more common as sensors get smaller, but M12 remains the most widely used industrial connector.
faqs.decisionGuide: M8 for compact sensors. M12 for general industrial applications.

categories.name: Custom Interconnect Solutions
categories.description: Bronze Tech custom interconnect solutions provide tailored connector designs for unique application requirements. Services include custom pin configurations, special materials and plating, unique form factors, and integrated cable assemblies. From concept to production, our engineering team delivers optimized interconnect solutions.
categories.longDescription: Bronze Tech custom interconnect solutions address specialized requirements that cannot be met with standard products. Our capabilities include custom pin counts and arrangements, specialized contact materials and plating (gold, palladium-nickel, selective plating), unique housing materials (high-temperature plastics, metal alloys), custom form factors and mounting options, integrated cable assemblies with overmolding, and application-specific testing and qualification. The custom design process includes requirements analysis, concept design, prototyping, testing and validation, and production ramp. Typical lead time is 8-12 weeks for custom designs. Applications include aerospace, medical devices, automotive, industrial equipment, and telecommunications.

selectionGuide.title: Custom Interconnect Design Process
selectionGuide.description: Guide to working with Bronze Tech on custom interconnect solutions

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for custom interconnect solutions?
faqs.answer: Typical lead time for custom interconnect solutions is 8-12 weeks from design approval to production. This includes: 2-3 weeks for tooling and initial samples, 2-3 weeks for testing and validation, and 4-6 weeks for production ramp. Lead time may vary based on complexity, material availability, and testing requirements. Rush services are available for urgent projects with 4-6 week lead time at additional cost. For simple modifications to existing products, lead time may be reduced to 4-6 weeks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for 8-12 weeks. Rush service available for urgent needs.

faqs.question: What information is needed to start a custom interconnect project?
faqs.answer: To start a custom interconnect project, provide: 1) Application description and operating environment, 2) Electrical requirements (voltage, current, signal type), 3) Mechanical constraints (size, mounting, mating cycles), 4) Environmental requirements (temperature, moisture, chemicals), 5) Regulatory requirements (UL, CE, automotive, medical), 6) Estimated annual volume and target cost, 7) Any existing connector preferences or reference designs. Our FAE team will review your requirements and provide a design proposal within 1 week.
faqs.decisionGuide: Gather all requirements before contacting. More details lead to better proposals.

products.name: Custom High-Voltage Board-to-Board
products.description: Custom 20-pin board-to-board connector designed for high-voltage applications up to 1000V with increased creepage distances
products.shortDescription: Custom high-voltage board-to-board connector with 1000V rating and enhanced creepage
descriptionParagraphs: The CUS-BTB-HV-20 is a custom-designed board-to-board connector for high-voltage power electronics applications.
This connector features increased creepage and clearance distances to support 1000V operation.
The custom design includes specialized insulation materials and contact spacing for safety and reliability.

faeReview.author: Dr. Thomas Anderson
faeReview.title: Principal Engineer - Custom Solutions
faeReview.content: The CUS-BTB-HV-20 demonstrates our capability to design connectors for demanding high-voltage applications. The 8mm creepage distance exceeds standard requirements for 1000V operation, providing safety margin for polluted environments. The PBT GF30 housing material offers excellent tracking resistance and thermal stability. This custom design was developed for a solar inverter customer and has proven reliable in field installations. For similar high-voltage needs, we can customize pitch, pin count, and voltage rating.
faeReview.highlight: Custom high-voltage design with enhanced safety margins

alternativeParts.reason: Fewer positions for simpler high-voltage designs

faqs.question: What makes CUS-BTB-HV-20 suitable for high-voltage applications?
faqs.answer: The CUS-BTB-HV-20 is designed specifically for high-voltage with several key features: 1) Increased creepage distance of 8mm (vs 4mm standard) prevents tracking across insulation surfaces. 2) Increased clearance of 6mm prevents arcing through air. 3) PBT GF30 housing material has excellent tracking resistance (CTI >600V). 4) 5.08mm pitch provides adequate spacing between contacts. 5) Special contact geometry reduces electric field concentration. These features allow safe operation at 1000V DC, exceeding standard connector ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Custom design for 1000V. Standard connectors limited to 250-300V.

faqs.question: Can CUS-BTB-HV-20 be customized for different voltage ratings?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CUS-BTB-HV series can be customized for various voltage ratings from 500V to 1500V. The design is scalable - higher voltages require larger creepage/clearance distances, which can be achieved by increasing pitch or adding barriers. For voltages above 1500V, different insulation materials and contact designs may be required. Contact our custom solutions team with your specific voltage, current, and dimensional requirements for a tailored design proposal.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for custom voltage ratings from 500V to 1500V.

faqs.question: What safety certifications does CUS-BTB-HV-20 have?
faqs.answer: The CUS-BTB-HV-20 carries UL and cUL recognition for 1000V operation. It is also VDE certified for IEC 60664-1 applications. The connector meets clearance and creepage requirements for overvoltage category III at 1000V. For specific application certifications (medical, automotive, etc.), additional testing may be required. Contact our team for certification documentation and to discuss any additional testing needs for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: UL/VDE certified for 1000V. Contact us for additional certifications.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity for CUS-BTB-HV-20?
faqs.answer: As a custom product, the CUS-BTB-HV-20 has a minimum order quantity (MOQ) of 1,000 pieces. This MOQ covers tooling amortization and setup costs. For larger volumes, pricing decreases significantly - contact us for volume pricing at 5K, 10K, and 50K+ quantities. For prototyping needs, we offer sample quantities (10-100 pieces) at NRE pricing. Lead time for production orders is 8-10 weeks after design approval.
faqs.decisionGuide: MOQ 1,000 pieces. Samples available for prototyping.

faqs.question: Is CUS-BTB-HV-20 suitable for outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The standard CUS-BTB-HV-20 is designed for indoor industrial applications. For outdoor use, we recommend the CUS-BTB-HV-20-OD version with enhanced sealing and UV-resistant housing material. The OD version includes additional gaskets and conformal coating for moisture protection. For extreme outdoor environments with temperature cycling and high humidity, consider adding a potting compound after assembly for complete environmental sealing. Contact our team for outdoor application recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard for indoor. Use OD version or potting for outdoor.

products.name: Custom Cable Assembly with Overmold
products.description: Custom cable assembly with integrated connector and overmolded strain relief for harsh industrial environments
products.shortDescription: Custom overmolded cable assembly with integrated connector for industrial use
descriptionParagraphs: The CUS-CAB-ASSY-01 is a custom cable assembly combining connector, cable, and overmolded strain relief in one integrated solution.
The overmolding process creates a seamless, watertight seal between connector and cable.
This design is ideal for applications requiring maximum reliability and environmental protection.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Park
faeReview.title: FAE - Cable Solutions
faeReview.content: The CUS-CAB-ASSY-01 showcases our integrated cable assembly capability. The overmolding process eliminates the weak point where cable meets connector, providing superior strain relief and environmental sealing. The high-flex PUR cable is excellent for continuous motion applications - I've seen these last 10+ million cycles in robotic installations. The IP68 rating is genuine - we've tested these submerged at 2 meters. For any application with vibration, motion, or harsh environments, overmolded assemblies are the way to go.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated overmolded design for maximum reliability

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost option for less demanding applications

companionParts.description: Panel mount receptacle to mate with cable assembly

faqs.question: What cable lengths are available for CUS-CAB-ASSY-01?
faqs.answer: CUS-CAB-ASSY-01 can be manufactured in any length from 0.5m to 50m. Standard lengths are 1m, 2m, 5m, and 10m with shorter lead times. Custom lengths are available with the same 8-10 week lead time. For very long runs (>20m), consider signal integrity requirements for your application - we can add shielding or use larger gauge conductors as needed. Contact us with your specific length and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard lengths 1-10m. Custom 0.5-50m available.

faqs.question: What is the minimum bend radius for CUS-CAB-ASSY-01?
faqs.answer: The minimum bend radius for CUS-CAB-ASSY-01 is 10 times the cable diameter (approximately 60mm for standard cable). This high-flex design allows installation in tight spaces and continuous motion applications. For static installations, maintain at least 5x bend radius. For continuous flex applications (robotic, cable carriers), use 15-20x bend radius for maximum life. The overmold area should not be bent - maintain straight section of at least 50mm from overmold.
faqs.decisionGuide: 10x diameter minimum. 15-20x for continuous flex.
